candidates.row_label: Petroleum and natural gas sales including royalty revenue
candidates.why: Represents gross petroleum and natural gas sales before deducting royalties; best match for total revenue/top-line.

candidates.row_label: Petroleum and natural gas revenue, net of royalties
candidates.why: Represents primary operating revenue after royalty deductions; useful as an alternative revenue label across years.

final_choice.row_label: Petroleum and natural gas sales including royalty revenue
Petroleum and natural gas revenue, net of royalties

variables.notes: Using gross sales as the primary REVT line and net-of-royalties revenue as a fallback label for other periods if needed.

candidates.why: Expense item but relates to receivables credit risk, not direct production costs; not appropriate for COGS.
Mixed or non-operating line, not specifically direct production or service costs.
Mixed or non-operating line, not specifically direct production or service costs.
Compensation-related overhead, more akin to SG&A than COGS and is also not clearly linked to direct production costs.
Balance sheet asset; not an income statement expense and therefore cannot represent COGS.

variables.notes: REVIEWED. None of the provided COGS candidates represent direct production/service costs. The row 'Direct operating' on the income statement is the standard oil & gas equivalent of COGS, so it is selected from outside the candidate list. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Direct operating

candidates.why: Explicit SG&A-type overhead category, separate from COGS and D&A, and populated in recent years.

variables.notes: Explicit G&A row exists and is populated, so it alone is used for SG&A components per the hierarchy rule.
No research & development expense line is apparent in the provided income statement; assume R&D is not separately reported.

candidates.why: Net interest and other income line, but includes non-interest items; use only if pure interest expense lines were absent.
Primary interest expense line; best representation of financing cost.
Net interest plus other income; not pure interest expense but can provide data if specific expense lines are missing for some years.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest component related to lease liabilities; a secondary, more specific interest expense line.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease-related interest; useful as an additional fallback label.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: Prioritized pure interest expense lines first; lease-related interest lines included as fallbacks. Net interest/other income lines were not selected because they mix interest with other items.

candidates.why: Income statement item; not an equity balance sheet account.
Income statement item; not an equity balance sheet account.
Equity attributable to shareholders; matches required book equity excluding any non-controlling interests.
Total equity; appears numerically identical to Shareholders Equity but less specific regarding ownership attribution.
Balance sheet total; includes liabilities and equity and thus is not suitable as BE.

variables.notes: Chose 'Shareholders Equity' as book equity attributable to owners; minority interests are not separately disclosed, so no adjustment is needed.
No line for non-controlling interests or minority interests appears on the balance sheet; MIB is treated as zero.
